cards.text: To use string streams, you must {{c1::#include <sstream>}}.
The three string stream classes are {{c1::std::istringstream}} (input), {{c2::std::ostringstream}} (output), and {{c3::std::stringstream}} (bidirectional).

cards.front: What is the primary difference between stringstream and cin/cout?
cards.back: Stringstreams provide a buffer but are not connected to an I/O channel (keyboard, monitor). They work with strings in memory.

cards.text: Two ways to put data into a stringstream: {{c1::os << "text"}} or {{c2::os.str("text")}}.
Two ways to get data from a stringstream: {{c1::os.str()}} returns the entire buffer, or {{c2::os >> var}} extracts values sequentially.

cards.question: What does this code output?
cards.explanation: Stringstreams can convert numbers to strings using the << operator and str() to retrieve the result.

cards.question: What does this code do?
cards.answer: Converts string "123 45.6" to int n=123 and double d=45.6
cards.explanation: Stringstreams can parse strings into numbers using the >> operator.

cards.front: What is the difference between str() and operator>> when extracting from a stringstream?
cards.back: str() returns the entire buffer contents. operator>> extracts values sequentially, advancing the stream position each time.

cards.text: To clear a stringstream buffer, use {{c1::ss.str("")}} or {{c2::ss.str(std::string{})}}. Also call {{c3::ss.clear()}} to reset error flags.

cards.front: Why should you call clear() after clearing a stringstream with str("")?
cards.back: clear() resets any error flags that may have been set and returns the stream to the ok state, ensuring it can be reused properly.

cards.explanation:
Stringstreams are useful for:
  • Converting between strings and numbers
  • Buffering output for display at a later time
  • Processing input line-by-line
  • Building formatted strings
examples: Safer than sprintf/sscanf for C++ code

cards.question: What's the output?
cards.explanation: str() returns the entire buffer content, even if >> has already extracted part of it. The stream position and buffer contents are independent.
